questions.question: Q1. Themes
questions.portal_action: Select one only after partner technical field is confirmed.
questions.draft: Preferred fit is Semiconductors only if the Swiss partner scope concerns AI compute, accelerators, photonic interconnects, sensing, or power and energy systems. Otherwise select Engineering Biology or Life Sciences only if the partner evidence supports that theme.

questions.question: Q2. Applicant location
questions.portal_action: Enter applicant details now if the portal section is open; add partners later.
questions.draft: R&A Consulting and Brokerage Services Ltd, company number NI696693, registered office 1 Quadrant Place, Belfast, Northern Ireland, BT12 4HX. Lead contact: Gary Anthony Leckey, gaxlec@gmail.com. Partner organisation names, full registered addresses, and any subcontractor details must be inserted after the Swiss implementation partner and research institute evidence is recorded.

questions.question: Q3. Animal testing
questions.portal_action: Select No only if the final partner technical scope does not introduce animal work.
questions.draft: No. The current Aureon evidence automation work package uses software workflows, source records, benchmark reporting, and audit trails. No animal trials or animal testing are included in the current UK work package.

questions.question: Q4. Permits and licences
questions.portal_action: Do not certify until the partner technical work is confirmed.
questions.draft: The current Aureon work package is software evidence automation and does not itself require sector permits or licences. The final answer must be confirmed after the Swiss implementation partner, research institute, and technical scope identify whether any regulated activity, test facility, controlled data, hardware, biological material, or export-controlled work is involved.

questions.question: Q5. International collaboration
questions.portal_action: Complete after partner names, countries, roles, and Innosuisse application status are recorded.
questions.draft: The intended collaboration is a UK-Swiss collaborative R&D project. R&A Consulting and Brokerage Services Ltd will provide the UK Aureon Evidence OS work package for evidence automation, reproducibility records, claim control, and funder-ready audit trails. The Swiss implementation partner, research institute or waiver condition, and any subcontractors must be named before finalising this answer.

questions.question: Q6. Export licence
questions.portal_action: Complete after export-control review of the final technical scope.
questions.draft: No export-controlled items are evidenced in the current Aureon software evidence work package. The final answer must be confirmed after the partner technical scope is fixed, especially if the selected theme involves semiconductors, sensing, advanced computing, biological systems, or dual-use technology.

questions.question: Q7. Trusted Research and Innovation
questions.portal_action: Complete after theme, partner, export-control, and NSI relevance are reviewed.
questions.draft: The project is an international collaboration and may fall within Trusted Research and Innovation review depending on the final theme. Aureon's current work package is evidence automation, workflow traceability, and audit reporting. It does not claim access to controlled hardware, biological systems, sensitive personal data, or dual-use material unless those facts are supplied by confirmed partners. The final answer must list any NSI areas, dual-use implications, export-control position, and controlled-list items after partner scope is confirmed.

questions.question: Q8. Need or challenge
questions.portal_action: Use as a base, then insert partner-specific technical need and market evidence.
questions.draft: Innovation teams in cross-border R&D generate evidence across technical records, partner reports, data exports, design notes, benchmark results, and funder obligations. The business need is a reliable method to keep project claims connected to source evidence throughout research, development, and exploitation planning. Current workflows often rely on manual document assembly and late-stage evidence clean-up, which weakens traceability, slows partner review, and increases the risk of unsupported claims. Aureon Evidence OS addresses this challenge by creating a structured evidence workflow for source inventories, claim classification, reproducible records, benchmark or deployment evidence, risk tagging, and funder-ready audit trails. The partner-specific technical need, market opportunity, and current limitations must be added once the Swiss implementation partner defines the qualifying theme.

questions.question: Q9. Approach and innovation
questions.portal_action: Use with the Q9 appendix after inserting partner technical module.
questions.draft: The project will combine the partner's qualifying technical R&D work with Aureon Evidence OS as the assurance and reproducibility layer. Aureon will define the evidence architecture, map claims to sources, maintain reproducible workflow records, and prepare structured reporting outputs for partner and funder review. The innovation is the controlled integration of evidence automation into the R&D lifecycle rather than treating assurance as an end-of-project reporting task. This improves reviewability, reduces unsupported claims, and helps the consortium maintain a common evidence base across UK and Swiss work packages. The final answer must specify the technical innovation in the selected theme, freedom-to-operate basis, expected demonstrator or process outputs, and how the partner technology improves on current alternatives.

questions.question: Q10. Team and resources
questions.portal_action: Use with the Q10 appendix after partner team evidence is recorded.
questions.draft: R&A Consulting and Brokerage Services Ltd will provide the Aureon evidence automation work package led by Gary Anthony Leckey. The UK role covers source inventory, claim classification, reproducibility records, risk tagging, reporting templates, and final evidence-pack preparation. The final project team must include the Swiss implementation partner, a research institute or documented waiver condition, and named technical, commercial, and project-management roles from each organisation. Facilities, equipment, test environments, datasets, hardware, biological materials, or other technical resources must be supplied and evidenced by the lead technical partner before final submission.

questions.question: Q11. Market awareness
questions.portal_action: Insert partner market data and selected theme market evidence.
questions.draft: The addressable market for Aureon's contribution is the growing need for auditable evidence workflows in collaborative R&D, technical assurance, and funder reporting. The partner-specific market must be defined by the qualifying theme and the Swiss implementation partner's product or service route. Aureon's role supports that market by improving evidence traceability, reducing review friction, and strengthening the consortium's ability to demonstrate technical progress and commercial readiness. The final answer must quantify the partner target market, customer segments, value chain, barriers to entry, UK position, Swiss contribution, and route to adoption.

questions.question: Q12. Outcomes and route to market
questions.portal_action: Insert partner route-to-market and IP position.
questions.draft: The expected Aureon outputs are an evidence register, claim-to-source map, reproducible workflow templates, risk and assumption register, partner review pack, technical reporting pack, and final audit trail. These outputs support the lead partner's route to market by making the technical case easier to review, reproduce, and evidence. The final route-to-market must identify the partner product or service, customer route, revenue or productivity model, commercial exploitation plan, IP ownership and use, research dissemination plan, and how UK and Swiss partners will use and protect project outputs.

questions.question: Q13. Wider impacts
questions.portal_action: Quantify after partner impact assumptions are available.
questions.draft: Aureon's work package can improve the reliability and transparency of R&D reporting, which benefits project partners, assessors, customers, and later investors by making claims easier to verify. Wider impacts may include stronger UK-Swiss collaboration, improved innovation governance, better reuse of project knowledge, and more efficient preparation of evidence for commercialisation. The final answer must quantify partner-specific economic benefits, productivity effects, regional benefits, environmental impacts, social impacts, skills effects, regulatory implications, and any negative impacts with mitigation.

questions.question: Q14. Project management
questions.portal_action: Use with the Q14 appendix after work-package costs and partner leads are confirmed.
questions.draft: The project should be managed through clearly assigned work packages, monthly partner reviews, an evidence register, milestone reporting, risk review, and change control. Aureon will own the evidence architecture, workflow traceability, assurance reporting, and final evidence pack. The final answer must show each work package, lead partner, cost, milestone dependencies, reporting lines, project-management tools, and how UK and Swiss work remains balanced under the competition rules.

questions.question: Q15. Risks
questions.portal_action: Use with the Q15 appendix after technical and partner risks are known.
questions.draft: Core risks include partner evidence delay, unclear technical scope, unsupported claims, weak cost balance, export-control uncertainty, IP/data-plan gaps, and insufficient evidence for commercial route-to-market. Aureon mitigates these through an evidence register, claim controls, source mapping, risk tagging, partner review packs, and staged submission controls. The final answer must add partner-specific technical, commercial, managerial, environmental, regulatory, ethical, and data risks with owners, probability, impact, and mitigation.

questions.question: Q16. Added value
questions.portal_action: Insert partner funding position and alternative investment evidence.
questions.draft: Public funding would allow the consortium to integrate evidence assurance into the R&D project from the start, reducing technical and commercial risk while improving the credibility of outputs for review and later exploitation. Without public funding, Aureon's work would likely remain a narrower software evidence prototype rather than a cross-border assurance layer tested inside a qualifying partner project. The final answer must explain how funding accelerates the partner technology, changes R&D activity for each organisation, supports investment readiness, and complements any existing or planned finance.

questions.question: Q17. Costs and value for money
questions.portal_action: Do not finalise until budgets, eligible costs, and partner contributions are evidenced.
questions.draft: The final answer must state total eligible project costs, UK grant request, partner contributions, finance sources, subcontractor costs, cost balance, and value for money. Current safe position: the UK grant funding request cannot exceed GBP450,000 and the draft should preserve the 18 to 36 month duration and 1 February 2027 start date. The final budget must respect the competition's partner and country balance rules and must be supported by source evidence.
